ref: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Klɛ Yohanɛsɛ badɔkpasɛ nɛ ba klɛ be bisi Yesu asɛ, “Kide so pɛɛ bulɔ anɛ Farisianɛ nɛ bunya onugbu pɛɛ, wɔnɔ nɛ wɔ badɔkpasɛ nɛ xũ nɔ tɔɔ nya onugbu?”
translated: John came to Jesus and said, "Why do the Pharisees eat bread and when they come to offer bread?"

ref: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
text: Pɔ onu gɛ bee vu nɔ, nɔ tɛ ga nɛ awɔ̃ nɛ kibui nɔ nɛ gɛ nɔ bɔ ɖɔ ɔdɔgɔsɛ kana gɛ nɔ bɔ ɖɔɛ lɛ. Ɔɖɔ lɛlɛ nɛ mɛ ba ba kɔ nɔ kibui nɔ nɛ gɛ nɔ bɔ ɖɔ,
translated: He sent them to speak to the people of Kibui, and told them not to go anywhere else until they reach Kibui and speak to the elders there. And when they arrived, they did not speak to anyone except the elders,

ref: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
text: Gɛ bedzi aŋa kɛdɔ nɛɛ, Yesu ayɛkɔ gbɛgbɛvu, ehu ahwɛ klɛ aya yɛmɛ, klɛ ayɛkɔ kɔ yɛ badɔkpasɛ, klɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Nɔ yɛkɛ tɛ nɔ tã, mɛ shuidzya nɛɛ lɛ.”
translated: When the time came, Jesus healed the blind, and seeing them, he went up to the crowd and said to them, "You have come out, so come here, let us eat together."

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: Yɛ ɛshã ɛlɛ dzi sɛ, “Nɔ tɛ yɛkɔ kɛdɔnyɛdɔ kɔ wɔnɔ kidzo gɛga nɛ ɔyɛkɔdɛ ovusi, nɔ tɛ yɛkɔ kɛŋɛŋa, nɔ tɛ yɛkɔ ɔkpɛtɛ nɛ kɔba nɛ ɔdzadi mɛ.
translated: They say the way is thus: "If you do not follow the path, you will not reach the town and you will not find peace; if you do not sow seeds, if you do not plant trees and harvest, you will not eat."

ref: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
text: Klɛ ashɛ̃ alɔ balɛ klɛ ekoeyi nɛ ɔmagbla nɛm avɛ Betani, ɔkɛlɛ gɛ ada za. Kabhɔ Tsitsi
translated: The small or big word of Ekoeyi in the town of Bethany, he went and a child was born there. Praise be to Tsitsi.

ref: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
text: So onu gɛ ayalete nɛ aba ba, kidee aba bhɛtɛ nɛ bagɛ tsri buya nɛa bha?”
translated: Did they go to the house, or are they eating the food there?

ref: Jesus asked his disciples if they understood all these things. They said, “Yes, we do.”
text: Yesu ebisi balɛ asɛ, “Sɛ no nu ebuia nɛ petee mɛ?” Be bu klɛ nu asɛ, “Ee.”
translated: Jesus said to them, "Who do you say I am?" They answered him, "Yes."

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Klɛ oli toli mɛ ode owũsise sɛ, “Meyi naa, gɛ ɛdɛmɛnɔ; yɛm mɛ mɔ awɔ̃kuku mɔmɔ.”
translated: He taught the word of Owusise, saying, “Meyi naa, come and take them; they are the ones who will inherit the earth.”

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Otɔsɛ ganɛ bubhui le bhui ewi alɛ nɛa bha agɔ, anɔnyanɔ agɛ ta fla gɔ, pɔ nɛ agɛ be ti bu kɔ lɔ nɛ so, ewi alɛlɛ nɛa bha aba tsɛ.
translated: When Otɔsɛ eats yam on the ground after eating, the poor takes it to the market, but if he has no money to buy, he goes home when done.

ref: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
text: ba pɛɛ ɛzakɔgbla nɛ bakplɔ̃ shu klɛ ba dɛmɛ bugbugbla mɛ zɛza nɛ ixogokɔevu nɛ mɛ,
translated: They spoke about the work and the carrying in the children's place and the movement of water and the person's guidance.

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: Yesu adɛ tsre yɛ banɔ nɛ kibuinyɛbui gɛ adɛ kpɛɛ lɛ kɛkpalɛ. Pɔ onu gɛ yɛ nɛ yɛ badɔkpasɛ nɛ tite bee li, adanɛ kibuinyɛbui mɛ petee akɔ balɛ. Yesu Ayɔ Kɛlɛgɛganɛ Nu
translated: Jesus said to teach you to bring the children to speak to them with love. But you hindered the small ones, for the kingdom belongs to such as these, and the children cannot enter the kingdom. Jesus, our Lord and Savior.

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
text: Pɔ onu gɛ anɔ teli aba kɔ sɛ beyi bhlia nɛ di gɛ ba yɛkɛ mɛa bha ŋa nɛ be de ku buzizi mɛ, aba pɛ kɔ yɛ adɛ onu gɛ ba yɛkɔ buhɔ gbla tsa kpɛ yɛ oli pɛ ba yɛkɔ ada tɛ̃ akpɛ aƒu odzudzukɔ.
translated: We also need to eat when the children are small and cannot eat properly, and we have to work hard until we can eat or until the food is finished.

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Gɛ owulago ɔna, ba yɛkɔ banɔ gelee gɛ ɛfufɔɛkpã za balɛa bha nɛ amɛna Yesu, klɛ akɛka ɛfufɔɛ nɛ ebu nɛ kibui tirikpo ko tɛɖuɖɔ klɛ ebeke banɔ nɛ petee banya.
translated: He left his home, and they followed him in the way of life to believe in Jesus, and they spoke about the way to enter the kingdom of heaven, teaching and loving others as well.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Klɛ kibe nɔɔ nɛ gɛ ɔsɛ okulukpaya, tɛbhɛtɛ ansɛ gɛ itroelete nɛ baa bhɛtɛ, alasɛ balɛ nɛɛ ba pɛɛa sɛ tɛ ba za kititi nɛ oxogokɔevu nɛm, anɛ edzogbla nɛa bha sɛ tɛ banɔ ba mɔ balɛ. Itsre nɔ kɛgba sɛ, balɛ nɛɛ di na tɛ yɛkɛ balɛ ahwɛhuhu nɛ petee.
translated: Klɛ did not eat the meal, and when they came to eat, the children wanted to eat again, but they had to take the small ones to the farm, and after cutting the head, they needed to speak. This one can carry it, they said, and they ate until they were full.

ref: That evening a rich disciple named Joseph from the town of Arimathea
text: Gɛ owulago nɛ ɔna, ehoeyielete teli ede Arimatea, gɛ be kulu asɛ Yosɛfu, gɛ yɛnahwɛ nɛ ebe dzi Yesu adɔkpasɛ.
translated: In the town of Arimathea, there lived a man named Joseph, and he took him to the place where Jesus was buried.

ref: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
text: Gɛ ba vɛ yɛ ɔkplasɔ kɔ, ba tsyanɔ anyɛ teli gɛ ede Sirene, gɛ be kulu asɛ Simɔnɛ nɛ kidzo nɛ mɛ, klɛ ba trɔ anyɛ nɛa bha sɛ tɛ ayɛkɛ butsiosi nɛ etuku akɔ yɛ.
translated: When they went to the market, they met Sirene, they took something to Simon with me, and then they traveled there when they arrived at the village after the long journey.

ref: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
text: Kɛlɛ so alasɛ wɔnɔ tsyɛ nɔ za tɛbɛbala mɛ, alasɛ Anɔdzyrame yɛ Eyi nɛ aba ba nɛ ɔɖɔ nɔɔ nɛ mɛ gɛ otoo di kidzoe nu.
translated: Kɛlɛ and Alasɛ went to Tɛbɛbala when Anɔdzyrame was there; Eyi is the father of aba, whose child's name is ɔɖɔ, and the house where they live there.

ref: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
text: Kɛlɛ so, anɔnyanɔ gɛ anyɛna shu ansɛ anuvɔ bhlia nɛɛ yɛ enu agbɛgbladɛ nɛ owũsise bukusiɔŋakɔ nɛ mɛ.
translated: When they went to fetch water, they saw a calabash and drank from it, and this calabash was at Owusise Bukusiongako.

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: “Nɔ tɛ bala wɔnɔ kɛkesrɔdɔ nɛ kesukpa nɛa bha, gɛ babɔ nɛ budzyewule lɛ gɛ ba zi kɛ lɛ, bezi tsyɛ be be zuruɛ kɛ lɛ.
translated: I did not eat the porridge and the soup here because they were bad, the father and the child also did not eat it, nobody was hungry so they left it.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Ɔkɛlɛ adzɛ nɛ gɛ enyi kɛdɔnɔ nɛ gɛ ba yɛa bha so nɛɛ, ekoeyi aba klɛ ebe zhiadɔ nɛ yɛ otugba, klɛ eshuku nɛ kifu, klɛ etsre yɛ kɛ gɛ ba nɛ yɛa bha.
translated: Kɛlɛ began and they said they would come to do that thing, then they came and saw the place where the dog was, and they ate the dog, and they did what they came to do.
